entry.title: Re: CSapEditiorCtrl::GetObject: Object 164 does not exist...
entry.content:
This issue occurs because the SAPscript editor is no longer part of the SAP GUI default installation selections

You need to download it separately. 

The Installer for SAPscript Legacy Text Editor can be downloaded from SAP Software Download Center:

For GUI 8.00:

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter

-> Support Packages and Patches
-> Click on tile 'Software Downloads' which starts the Lauchpad
-> In the header select 'SUPPORT PACKAGES & PATCHES'
-> Select 'By Category'
-> SAP Frontend Components
-> SAPSCRIPT LEGACY TEXT EDITOR
-> SCR LEGACY TEXT EDITOR GUI8.00

The downloaded installer can be installed locally or can be included to SAP GUI Installation Server for installation and centralized distributed.

The extending of the existing SAP GUI Installation Server Configuration can be done by following steps:

- Download 'SAPSCRIPT LEGACY TEXT EDITOR' from SAP Software Download Center
- Extract the installer’s content in one folder, by calling the installer file from command line with the option /extract=”<FOLDER_NAME>”  
     e.g. SCRLTESP76000_0-80004481.EXE /extract=”SAPscriptEditor”
  (This example will create a folder named ”SAPscriptEditor” and extract the installer’s content into it.)
- Start NWSAPSetupAdmin.exe from the SETUP directory of the installation server
- Push the Import Products in toolbar
- Follow the wizard and supply the path to the source containing the extracted product to be added to the installation server.
    e.g. SAPscriptEditor/Setup

SAPscript Legacy Text Editor is an obsolete component and is replaced by "MS Word as SAPScript Text Editor". 
More information about "MS Word as SAPScript Text Editor" you can in the note 742662.

entry.title: Re: 599803/2026 follow up...unable to determine how to im...
entry.content:
Hello,
I am not sure was able to understand the requirement. However, for implementing custom logic in UKM_FILL, inside method IF_EX_UKM_FILL~FILL_FIELDS, you could change IS_NOTIFICATION-DATE and the date inside IS_NOTIFICATION-OBJKEY. Then add the entry in IS_NOTIFICATION to Et_xi_msg. That way, the credit exposure should be updated with the changed value.
You may check the method IF_EX_UKM_FILL~FILL_FIELDS in example class CL_EXM_IM_UKM_FILL to get an idea on how we can loop through IS_NOTIFICATION, change a value if needed and then pass it to et_xi_msg.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for Automatic HU Packing during GR for...
entry.content:
Good day @kkumara 

Thank you for your question.

For automatic Hu packing during Goods Receipt from a Production Order, the relevant standard scenario is Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt.

In simple terms you need to know the following:

  • Automatic Hu packing is not controlled only from the material master.
  • The production GR must be relevant to warehouse.
  • The storage location must be assigned to Warehouse Management.
  • You must use packing instructions or the supported automatic packing setup to maintain the packing logic.
  • If the warehouse production integration scope is not active, automatic HU packing during production GR will not work.

The main configuration and master data check you need to do is as follows:

  1. Check whether warehouse production integration with synchronous goods receipt is active.
  2. Check whether automatic goods receipt is active through the production scheduling profile or operation control key.
  3. The production order GR storage location must be the warehouse managed storage location mapped to the warehouse. If the order receives into a non warehouse managed storage location, HU packing in warehouse processing will not be triggered.
  4. Maintain the packing instruction or automatic packing rule for the finished product and packaging material. This ensures the system know how the finished product is packed into HU.
  5. Make sure the finished product and packaging material are extended to the required plant and warehouse related views where applicable.

Have a look at these references for more guidance.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-warehouse-management/integrating-warehouse-production-with-synchronous-goods-receipt_ab633927-d2f1-4ce2-8de7-7d9fa6581f05?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e003b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Great breakdown on the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SMB go-lives in the Hyderabad market, I'd add one critical nuance: the go-live week is where Hyderabad SMBs lose the most money, and it's almost never a technical issue — it's a data readiness and visibility problem.

Three failure patterns I see repeatedly:

1. Master data validation is skipped under time pressure.
Teams migrate from Tally/legacy spreadsheets and the item master is "good enough" on cutover night. Three weeks later, inventory reports are useless because unit of measure conversions weren't standardized. The fix: run a parallel inventory count in the old system AND SAP B1 for 2 weeks before go-live. Any variance >2% stops go-live until resolved. Without clean master data, you have no real-time visibility — you're just moving the same bad data into a new system.

2. User training is treated as a checkbox.
SMB owners assume the consultant will "handle it." What actually happens: 3 PowerPoint slides, a rushed demo, and a team that can log in but can't process a goods receipt. The result: workarounds that corrupt data, followed by 6 months of cleanup. Better model: identify 2–3 power users per department in week 1. Train them deeply. They become your go-live support army.

3. No cutover rollback plan.
If something breaks on go-live night, what's the decision tree? Who decides to rollback vs. push through? This sounds obvious but almost no SMB implementation documents this. The result: 48 hours of chaos before the inevitable rollback — which costs more than if you'd planned for it.

The go-live checklist I use for every Hyderabad implementation:

  • Parallel run confirmation (both systems running simultaneously for at least 5 business days)
  • Rollback decision criteria — documented, with named decision owner
  • Data freeze window (no master data changes 48 hours before cutover)
  • Go-live support team: internal champion + consultant available for 72 hours straight
  • First-week close check: finance runs month-end in SAP B1 within 5 business days of go-live
  • Batch cost gap analysis: For manufacturing and pharma clients, we now run an AI-assisted batch cost variance check in the first week — catches data migration gaps before they compound into month-end surprises

The single best investment: A formal 3-day parallel run before go-live where the team processes every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful? Yes. Expensive? Yes. Cheaper than data recovery after a bad cutover? Far less.

The teams that come to go-live with clean, validated data and real-time visibility dashboards ready from Day 1 close their first month-end in 3–4 days. Teams that skip this average 3–4 weeks of post-go-live disruption.

What specific module are you struggling with most during go-live? Happy to go deeper on any of these.

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs | TekAI: AI-powered operations intelligence on SAP B1

entry.title: Re: Bad Data Product, Bad AI - Now What?
entry.content:
I would say the AI Model. So the Architect with Data Engineer? Product Owner should be involved as well. 

Data Products can be flawed. The kind-of-support driven approach. Quickly finish and go for next, when it breaks be faaaar away :). Product owner, business accepts solutions even if not fully tested and even with flaws. Partially no time to test, providing least experienced for testing and not really knowing what is wanted. No AI as of now can provide the content. Therefore the experience matters, but this is difficult explain to cost-driven-management :). 

So, more or less, product owner accepted, but the Data Engineering Team must expect flawed sources and must ensure proper testing of the model I would say,.

entry.title: Possible issue in Alternative Account Determination after renumbering YCOA
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have recently performed a renumbering of the YCOA  in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

My concern is related to the impact this renumbering might have on the Alternative Account Determination.

As you can see in the screenshot below, the accounts have changed from their original numbers to new ones after the renumbering:

Will the existing configuration in Alternative Account Determination still work as expected with the new account numbers?

Do we need to review or adjust the mappings manually after the renumbering process?

When going into the Manage Chart of Accounts app and opening YCPT (which in our case is the alternative chart of accounts for Portugal), the list appears empty. However, when navigating to G/L Account Master Data, the accounts are there.

Two questions from this:

  1. If we post normal journal entries using the primary YCOA and then filter by alternative account, could we run into issues given that YCPT appears empty in the Manage Chart of Accounts app?
  2. When running statutory reports such as SAF-T, financial statements, VAT, and balance sheets - could the alternative account mapping cause problems here, or will the reports still work correctly regardless of YCPT appearing empty in that view?

Any insights or experiences on this topic would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to Configure Asset Disposal Posting (French Requirement – Separate NBV and Sales Revenue)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently implementing SAP for our subsidiary in France and have a question regarding the accounting treatment for fixed asset selling.
Under French accounting rules, when a fixed asset is disposed of, it is required to post:

The selling of the asset (acquisition cost and accumulated depreciation)
The net book value (NBV)
The sales amount (revenue)

These need to be recognized separately.
For example:

Acquisition cost: 1,000 EUR
Accumulated depreciation: 700 EUR
Net book value (NBV): 300 EUR
Sales price: 500 EUR

The expected accounting entries are:
1) Asset retirement
Dr Accumulated Depreciation 700
Dr Net Book Value (Expense) 300
Cr Acquisition Cost 1,000

2) Asset sale
Dr Accounts Receivable 500
Cr Sales price 500

(Then the gain/loss is reflected separately through the NBV and revenue accounts.)
However, we are not sure how to configure SAP (FI-AA) to achieve this type of posting, especially:

Separating NBV and sales revenue (instead of directly posting gain/loss)
Posting acquisition cost and accumulated depreciation correctly at the same time

Could you please advise:

Which configuration settings are required (e.g., account determination, transaction types)?
Whether this scenario can be handled using standard SAP functionality?
If any specific transaction types or posting variants should be used?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: selection in practice mode does not work the first time
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

This is unusual behavior so something is not correct.  I can think of one thing that is worth checking before you should raise a ticket to get support involved.

If you open one of the Simulations causing an issue in Producer and click on an action macro that does not play correctly in Practice Mode, is the Action value set to Left Click or something else?  It should be set to Left Click if you want it to behave normally.

 

 

If the value is set to Left Click but you are still getting the errors, please raise a support ticket using Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and have support take a look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM - personal data
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thanks for contacting SAP Support.
I checked your description, it seemed that you have found the overview KBA ##3429058 already. But for the detailed information about personal data collection for each application, please kindly hear from experts of each different component by separate cases.
For example, if you want to check personal data collection about Change and Deployment Management, please check with component SV-CLM-IMP-FTR.

Relevant components are as below:
 

Integration Monitoring                                  SV-CLM-OP-IM

Synthetic User Monitoring                            SV-CLM-OP-SUM

Real User Monitoring                                     SV-CLM-OP-RUM    

Configuration & Security Analysis               SV-CLM-OP-CSA

Operation Automation                                  SV-CLM-OP

Change and Deployment Management     SV-CLM-IMP-FTR

Job & Automation Monitoring                     SV-CLM-OP-JM

Intelligent Event Processing                         SV-CLM-OP-IEP  

Business Process Monitoring                       SV-CLM-OP-BPM   

 

##3429058 - Data Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM

 

Best regards,

Haterm
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Our RISE systems are not visible in our SAP Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
In RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard, only the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems with software component S4CORE are supported. 
All other system type like SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA Foundation are not supported.

  • Under the System View, only the Production, Test, and Development system type are visible. A demo S/4HANA system will not be displayed.
  • Under the Operations View, only those productive clients associated with the productive systems (technical) that appear in the System View can be displayed.

For more details, please review the following SAP help portal guide:

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/faq-system-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/faq-operations-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/system-view
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view

entry.title: "Post Revaluation" Function Availability & Documentation Gap
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are reaching out because we have received guidance from SAP OSS that conflicts with what we observe in our system, and we have been unable to find any official documentation to clarify the situation. We would greatly appreciate input from community members who may have encountered the same issue.

Issue 1: "Post Revaluation" function stated as currently unavailable We received the following response from SAP OSS: "What I mean is that 'Post Revaluation' is not yet available and will be a functional improvement in the future. If you want to do the Revaluation Posting, please use the 'Other Transactions' option."

The "Post Revaluation" menu item is visible in our Fixed Asset Management menu in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We expected this menu item to be a fully functional feature. However, OSS has informed us that it is not yet available and is planned as a future enhancement. This is unexpected, as the menu item is present in the system without any visible restriction or warning.

Issue 2: No documentation found to support this restriction We have searched SAP Help Portal, SAP Notes, and KBA databases, but we cannot find any official documentation that:

  • States that "Post Revaluation" is currently unavailable or restricted in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Confirms that "Other Transactions" is the officially recommended workaround for revaluation posting
  • Provides a planned release timeline for when "Post Revaluation" will become fully functional

The absence of documentation makes it very difficult to justify the use of a workaround internally, or to plan for when the standard function will be available.

Our Questions:

  1. Is there an official SAP Note or KBA that confirms the current unavailability of the "Post Revaluation" function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, could you please share the Note/KBA number?
  2. Is "Other Transactions" the officially supported and recommended workaround for revaluation posting at this time? Are there any known limitations or caveats when using this approach?
  3. Is there a planned release timeline for when "Post Revaluation" will become fully available? Which release version is it targeted for?
  4. Why is this limitation not documented in SAP Help Portal or other official SAP publications? If this is a Cloud-specific restriction, should it not be clearly stated in the product documentation?

We would appreciate a clear and direct response to each of the above questions, as we need official written confirmation to proceed with our internal processes and planning.

Thank you for your support.

##FI-A
##Fixed Asset Accountin
##asset-revaluatio
##post-revaluatio
##fixed-assets

entry.title: Re: Customer Unable to Raise Support Ticket

I understand your end customer, * is unable to create cases in SAP for Me. I reviewed our backend and do not see a valid contract associated with this customer account. The current status shows: Customer is without Maintenance and can´t use SAP for Me. I have attached a reference so you can see the same status I am seeing. 

Regarding to the issue , please kindly contact your Account Manager or SAP Order Management via sapcontractcenter@sap.com:

My product support team can only tell you if your end customer have a valid contract with SAP, we can't fix issues like this. Therefore, please work directly with SAP Order Management (contracts) and/or your SAP Account Manager to check your end customer'saccount and make any necessary updates. After their account is updated and authorized for the product, your end customer will be able to use it.

entry.title: Re: Custom App Interface Publishing Issues of ABAP Develo...
entry.content:
if you want to create active instance directly, you need to send IsActiveEntity=true in your payload for creation.

generally fiori UI never send IsActiveEntity=true, so you can use that to differentiate if the call is from external system or UI.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Report...
entry.content: I have developed a VB.NET report runner using Crystal Reports Runtime 13.0.35. But I am having issues specifically with updating the subreports. The runner dynamically changes reports from the design server: DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 to the runtime server: BRPSQL1 using RAS ReplaceConnection() and additional DataSourceConnection.SetConnection() calls. Facts established: 1. Main report connection successfully changes to BRPSQL1. 2. Embedded subreport "Boning Room Input.rpt" contains 3 SQL tables: * rptYieldCarcaseDetails * ProductMaster * ProductDescriptions 3. Runtime logging confirms: * subDoc.DataSourceConnections(0).SetConnection(BRPSQL1, Meat_Plant, ...) * DataSourceConnections(0).ServerName = BRPSQL1 * DataSourceConnections(0).DatabaseName = Meat_Plant 4. Runtime logging also confirms all subreport tables are updated: VERIFY SERVER = BRPSQL1 VERIFY DATABASE = Meat_Plant for all three tables. 5. Crystal RAS reports: OLD CONNECTION COUNT = 1 6. Despite all logged connections showing BRPSQL1, the viewer still behaves as follows: * First login prompt = BRPSQL1 * Credentials entered * Second login prompt = DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 * Logon Failed 7. If the Boning Room Input subreport is removed from the main report, the second login prompt disappears. 8. The subreport is NOT an on-demand subreport. 9. Database Expert shows standard SQL tables under: DESKTOP-94TBRUD\MSSQLSERVER01 Question: Has anyone seen a Crystal subreport continue prompting for the original design-time server after: * ReplaceConnection() * DataSourceConnections.SetConnection() * Table ApplyLogOnInfo() all report success and all table/server verification logging shows the new server? What other Crystal objects can retain a saved datasource/login independently of the tables and DataSourceConnections collection?

We have a requirement to perform a S4 Conversion upgrade from EHP8 to S4Hana 2023.

On-Prem
Database: HANA
OS:SLES
Database size:500 GB

Target:
S4Hana 2023.

No DMO needed only S4 Conversion.

How we can calculate SUM folder space requirement before hand? I also checked Sizing guide could not find any

Kindly do the needful

Thanks in advance

Regards,
Mithran

entry.title: Mass Print HU Labels frm the SAP EWM Warehouse Monitor-A Complete Techno-Functional Deployment Guide
entry.content:
⚠  Version & Release Disclaimer

This guide was developed and validated on SAP S/4HANA 2022 (FPS00) with Embedded EWM.

Field names, function module parameter names, internal table types, and BAdI method signatures can and do differ across EWM releases:

  •  S/4HANA 2020 Embedded EWM

  •  S/4HANA 2022 Embedded EWM  ←  this guide

  •  S/4HANA 2023 Embedded EWM

  •  S/4HANA 2025 Embedded EWM

  •  SCM-based (Decentralized) EWM 9.x

Before deploying any object from this guide in a different release, cross-check:

  1.  All TYPE references in DATA declarations against your system's data dictionary (SE11)

  2.  FM /SCWM/HU_READ_MULT parameter names and table types via SE37

  3.  BAdI /SCWM/EX_WME_CTRL method signature in SE18

  4.  Table /SAPAPO/MATKEY availability (used for material ID → material number resolution)

  5.  SSF_OPEN and SSF_CLOSE parameter lists — some parameters are version-specific

Introduction

In high-volume warehouse operations running on SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), Handling Unit (HU) label printing is a routine but operationally significant task. The standard SAP approach requires operators to navigate to individual HUs and trigger label printing one at a time — a workflow that becomes a measurable productivity constraint at scale.

This blog documents the complete end-to-end deployment of a custom SAP EWM enhancement that extends the Warehouse Monitor (/SCWM/MON) to support Mass Print of HU labels — allowing operators to select multiple HUs simultaneously and generate all labels in a single spool job.

System: SAP S/4HANA 2022 Embedded EWM. The guide covers architecture, all ABAP objects, complete FM source code, SPRO functional monitor configuration, and 10 iterative issues encountered and resolved during development.

Business Requirement

Warehouse operators needed the ability to select multiple Handling Units simultaneously from the EWM Monitor and trigger label printing for all of them in one action. This reduces manual effort, eliminates repetitive navigation, and improves throughput during high-volume inbound and outbound processing.

Technical Architecture

Component

Technology

Purpose

 

SPRO: Define Object Class Methods

Monitor configuration IMG

Registers FM as HU object class action (Method Z00001)

 

SPRO: Define Methods Presentation

Monitor configuration IMG

Assigns action to SAP monitor, all warehouses, Seq 15

 

BAdI /SCWM/EX_WME_CTRL

EWM BAdI framework

Passes selected HU rows and warehouse to the FM

 

Print Dialog

ABAP Screen 100 (Modal Dialog)

User selects printer and SmartForm name

 

Print Controller

FM ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON

Orchestrates the full 12-step print loop

 

Label Template

SmartForm ZHU_LABEL_PNG

Defines HU label layout and content

 

Spool Output

SSF_OPEN / SSF_CLOSE

Creates single consolidated spool job for all HUs

 

Objects Created

 

Object Type

Object Name

Description

 

Function Group

ZWM_FG_MONITOR_HU

EWM HU Mass Print Monitor Function Group

 

Function Module

ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON

Main FM — orchestrates mass print loop

 

Include

LZWM_FG_MONITOR_HUTOP

Global data declarations

 

Include

LZWM_FG_MONITOR_HUI01

Screen PAI/PBO modules

 

Include

LZWM_FG_MONITOR_HUUXX

Function module source code

 

Screen 100

SAPLZWM_FG_MONITOR_HU

Printer and form selection dialog (Modal)

 

GUI Status

SCREEN_100

PF keys: PRINT and CANCEL

 

GUI Title

MASS_PRINT_TITLE

Title: Mass Print — Select Printer and Form

 

SmartForm

ZHU_LABEL_PNG

HU label output template

 

Test Program

ZTEST_HU_LABEL

Unit test for SmartForm standalone execution

 

Monitor Method

Z00001 (Obj. Class HU)

SPRO entry linking FM to HU object class

 

 

Step-by-Step Deployment

 

Step 1 — Create Function Group (SE37)

  • SE37 → Function Groups → Create Function Group
  • Function Group: ZWM_FG_MONITOR_HU
  • Description: EWM HU Mass Print Monitor Function Group
  • Package: $TMP for sandbox; assign transport package for productive deployment
  • Save and activate

 

Step 2 — Global Data Include (LZWM_FG_MONITOR_HUTOP)

Open in SE38. These declarations are shared across all modules in the function group.

DATA: gv_printer TYPE rspopname,

      gv_form    TYPE tdsfname,

      ok_code    TYPE sy-ucomm,

      gv_lgnum   TYPE /scwm/lgnum.

 

Step 3 — Create Screen 100 (SE51)

Program: SAPLZWM_FG_MONITOR_HU, Screen 100. Screen Type: Modal Dialog Box.

  • Input field: GV_PRINTER (length 30, label: Printer:)
  • Input field: GV_FORM (length 30, label: Form Name:)
  • Hidden OK field: OK_CODE
  • Pushbutton: PRINT (function code PRINT)
  • Pushbutton: CANCEL (function code CANCEL)

 

Step 4 — GUI Status and Title (SE41)

  • GUI Status SCREEN_100: assign function codes PRINT and CANCEL to F-keys
  • GUI Title MASS_PRINT_TITLE: title text = 'Mass Print - Select Printer and Form'

 

Step 5 — Screen PAI/PBO Modules (LZWM_FG_MONITOR_HUI01)

MODULE STATUS_0100 OUTPUT.

  SET PF-STATUS 'SCREEN_100'.

  SET TITLEBAR 'MASS_PRINT_TITLE'.

ENDMODULE.

MODULE USER_COMMAND_0100 INPUT.

  CASE ok_code.

    WHEN 'PRINT'.

      IF gv_printer IS INITIAL. RAISE PRINT_CANCELLED. ENDIF.

      LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.

    WHEN 'CANCEL'.

      CLEAR gv_printer. CLEAR gv_form.

      LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.

  ENDCASE.

ENDMODULE.

Step 6 — Function Module ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON (SE37) — Full Source Code

Import parameters: IV_LGNUM (TYPE /SCWM/LGNUM, pass by value), IT_DATA (TYPE STANDARD TABLE).

Exceptions: NO_HU_SELECTED, PRINT_CANCELLED, PRINTER_INVALID, SMARTFORM_ERROR.

 

⚠  Release note: Validate all TYPE references, FM parameter names, and table /SAPAPO/MATKEY against your specific EWM release before activating. S/4HANA 2020, 2023, and 2025 may differ.

 

FUNCTION ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON.

*"------------------------------------------------------

  DATA: lt_hu_hdr       TYPE /scwm/tt_huhdr_int,

        ls_hu_hdr       TYPE /scwm/s_huhdr_int,

        lt_hu_itm       TYPE /scwm/tt_huitm_int,

        lt_huident_loc  TYPE /scwm/tt_huident,

        ls_itm          TYPE /scwm/s_huitm_int,

        lv_matnr        TYPE char18,

        lv_maktx        TYPE char40,

        lv_quan         TYPE char20,

        lv_meins        TYPE char3,

        lv_batch        TYPE char10,

        lv_fm_name      TYPE rs38l_fnam,

        ls_ctrl_para    TYPE ssfctrlop,

        ls_print_options TYPE ssfcompop,

        lv_count        TYPE i.

  " Step 1: Validate input

  IF it_data IS INITIAL.

    RAISE NO_HU_SELECTED.

  ENDIF.

  " Step 2: Copy generic table into typed HU identifier table

  lt_huident_loc = it_data.

  " Step 3: Read HU details and items

  CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/HU_READ_MULT'

    EXPORTING

      it_huident   = lt_huident_loc

      iv_lgnum     = iv_lgnum

    IMPORTING

      et_huhdr     = lt_hu_hdr

      et_huitm     = lt_hu_itm

    EXCEPTIONS

      hu_not_found   = 1

      internal_error = 2

      OTHERS         = 3.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

            WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

  " Step 4: Show printer/form dialog

  CLEAR: gv_printer, gv_form, ok_code.

  CALL SCREEN 100 STARTING AT 10 5 ENDING AT 70 15.

  IF gv_printer IS INITIAL OR gv_form IS INITIAL.

    RAISE PRINT_CANCELLED.

  ENDIF.

  " Step 5: Get SmartForm FM name

  CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'

    EXPORTING  formname         = gv_form

    IMPORTING  fm_name          = lv_fm_name

    EXCEPTIONS no_form          = 1

               no_function_module = 2

               OTHERS           = 3.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

    RAISE SMARTFORM_ERROR.

  ENDIF.

  " Step 6: Sort HUs ascending

  SORT lt_hu_hdr BY huident ASCENDING.

  " Step 7: Set print options

  ls_print_options-tdprinter = gv_printer.

  ls_print_options-tddest    = gv_printer.

  ls_print_options-tdnoprev  = 'X'.

  ls_print_options-tdcopies  = 1.

  " Step 8: Set control parameters (set BEFORE SSF_OPEN - do NOT reset inside loop)

  ls_ctrl_para-no_dialog = 'X'.

  ls_ctrl_para-getotf    = ' '.

  ls_ctrl_para-preview   = ' '.

  ls_ctrl_para-langu     = sy-langu.    " Required - blank causes internal_error

  ls_ctrl_para-device    = 'PRINTER'.   " Required - missing causes spool error

  " Step 9: Open spool - ONCE before the loop (single consolidated spool job)

  CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_OPEN'

    EXPORTING

      output_options    = ls_print_options

      control_parameters = ls_ctrl_para

    EXCEPTIONS

      formatting_error = 1

      internal_error   = 2

      send_error       = 3

      user_canceled    = 4

      OTHERS           = 5.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.

    RAISE SMARTFORM_ERROR.

  ENDIF.

  " Step 10: Print each HU

  LOOP AT lt_hu_hdr INTO ls_hu_hdr.

    CLEAR: ls_itm, lv_matnr, lv_maktx, lv_quan, lv_meins, lv_batch.

    READ TABLE lt_hu_itm INTO ls_itm

      WITH KEY guid_parent = ls_hu_hdr-guid_hu.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.

      " Resolve material number from EWM material ID (GUID)

      " NOTE: Field names and table /SAPAPO/MATKEY may differ in other EWM releases

      IF ls_itm-matid IS NOT INITIAL.

        SELECT SINGLE matnr INTO lv_matnr

          FROM /sapapo/matkey WHERE matid = ls_itm-matid.

        IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_matnr IS NOT INITIAL.

          SELECT SINGLE maktx INTO lv_maktx

            FROM makt WHERE matnr = lv_matnr AND spras = sy-langu.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

      IF ls_itm-batchid IS NOT INITIAL.

        lv_batch = |{ ls_itm-batchid }|.

      ENDIF.

      WRITE ls_itm-quan TO lv_quan. CONDENSE lv_quan.

      lv_meins = ls_itm-meins.

    ENDIF.

    TRY.

      CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name

        EXPORTING

          control_parameters = ls_ctrl_para

          output_options     = ls_print_options

          is_huhdr           = ls_hu_hdr

          iv_lgnum           = iv_lgnum

          iv_matnr           = lv_matnr

          iv_maktx           = lv_maktx

          iv_quan            = lv_quan

          iv_meins           = lv_meins

          iv_batch           = lv_batch

        EXCEPTIONS

          formatting_error   = 1

          internal_error     = 2

          send_error         = 3

          user_canceled      = 4

          OTHERS             = 5.

      " Suppress SSFCOMPOSER 150 (empty SmartForm - sandbox only)

      IF sy-subrc <> 0 AND

         NOT ( sy-msgid = 'SSFCOMPOSER' AND sy-msgno = '150' ).

        MESSAGE |Error: { sy-msgid } { sy-msgno } { sy-msgv1 }| TYPE 'W'.

      ENDIF.

    CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).

      MESSAGE |SmartForm error: { lx_error->get_text( ) }| TYPE 'W'.

    ENDTRY.

  ENDLOOP.

  " Step 11: Close spool

  CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_CLOSE'

    EXCEPTIONS

      formatting_error = 1

      internal_error   = 2

      send_error       = 3

      OTHERS           = 4.

  " Step 12: Success message

  DESCRIBE TABLE lt_hu_hdr LINES lv_count.

  MESSAGE |{ lv_count } HU(s) sent to printer { gv_printer } successfully.| TYPE 'S'.

ENDFUNCTION.

Step 7 — SmartForm ZHU_LABEL_PNG (SMARTFORMS)

Import parameters: IV_LGNUM, IS_HUHDR (TYPE /SCWM/S_HUHDR_INT), IV_MAKTX, IV_MATNR, IV_MEINS, IV_BATCH, IV_QUAN.

Structure: %PAGE1 → MAIN window → HU_LABEL text node. Output fields: HU identifier, product code, description, quantity, unit, batch.

Important: Do NOT add barcode elements requiring GS1/business partner number ranges unless

the system is configured with a GS1 company prefix. This triggers SSFCOMPOSER error 150

or business partner runtime errors that are difficult to diagnose without this context.

Step 8 — Monitor Configuration (SPRO — No ABAP Required)

This step makes the action visible in the monitor. Without it, all ABAP objects are complete but the button will not appear for users.

SPRO path: SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Monitoring → Warehouse Management Monitor → Define Object Class Methods

SPRO navigation: Monitoring → Warehouse Management Monitor → Define Object Class Methods

8.1 — Define Object Class Methods

Field

Value

Obj. Class

HU

Method

Z00001

Method Funct. Module

ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON

MethodIcon

(printer icon — select from F4 help)

AutoRefrsh

Leave unchecked

Visibility

Always Visible

Text

Mass Print HU labels

Presentation Text

Mass Print HU

Define Methods: Obj. Class HU | Method Z00001 | FM ZWM_HU_MASS_PRINT_MON | Always Visible

8.2 — Define Methods Presentation

Click Define Methods Presentation in the Dialog Structure left panel. Create a new entry:

Field

Value

Warehouse (War...)

**** (four asterisks — all warehouses)

Monitor

SAP (standard SAP monitor)

Obj. Class

HU

Sequence

15

Method

Z00001

Text

Mass Print HU labels

Dialog

0

Mthd Pres.

3 — Accumulated methods menu button (right-click context menu)

Define Methods Presentation: **** warehouses | SAP monitor | HU object class | Seq 15 | Accumulated menu button

Configuration result:

  •  Action visible in right-click context menu on ALL HU nodes

  •  Standard SAP monitor (/SCWM/MON → SAP monitor)

  •  All warehouses (**** = system-wide)

  •  Sequence 15 — placed after standard SAP actions in the menu

  •  Type 3 (Accumulated methods menu button) = right-click context entry

8.3 — Runtime Result

After saving SPRO configuration, open /SCWM/MON, navigate to Documents → Handling Unit, right-click any HU row. The Mass Print HU labels option appears in the context menu.

Upon clicking Print Button, the message saying 5 labels are sent to printer is displayed as below:

Runtime: Mass Print HU labels in right-click context menu, standard SAP monitor, Handling Unit node

Step 9 — End-to-End Test Procedure

Step

Action / Expected Result

1 — Open Monitor

/SCWM/MON → SAP monitor → Documents → Handling Unit

2 — Select HUs

Select one or more HU rows using the checkbox column

3 — Right-click

Right-click on a selected row to open the context menu

4 — Select Mass Print

Click 'Mass Print HU labels'

5 — Enter Print Details

Enter printer name and form name ZHU_LABEL_CAG in the popup

6 — Confirm

Click Print button

7 — Verify Status Bar

'X HU(s) sent to printer [name] successfully'

8 — Verify Spool

SP01 — spool requests visible for each HU

Key Issues Resolved During Development (S/4HANA 2022 EWM)

The following issues were encountered on S/4HANA 2022 Embedded EWM. Some may not occur on other releases; others may present differently. In all cases the root cause required analysis rather than trial and error.

 

#

Issue

Root Cause

Fix Applied

1

RSPO_R_RSPOPNAME_CHECK dump

FM unavailable in S/4HANA 2022 EWM

Removed FM call; used IS INITIAL check

2

SSF_OPEN JOB_OUTPUT_INFO unknown

Parameter absent in this system version

Removed IMPORTING job_output_info

3

Control params reset in loop

ls_ctrl_para overwritten each iteration

Moved all settings before SSF_OPEN

4

Quantity showing long decimal

Numeric type formatted as full decimal

WRITE TO + CONDENSE

5

Internal error — language blank

ls_ctrl_para-langu not populated

ls_ctrl_para-langu = sy-langu

6

Spool error — device missing

ls_ctrl_para-device not set

ls_ctrl_para-device = 'PRINTER'

7

Business partner runtime error

Broken barcode node needing GS1 setup

Deleted node from SmartForm

8

SSFCOMPOSER 150 — empty form

No printable content nodes

Suppressed in exception handler

9

CX_SY_DYN_CALL_PARAM_MISSING

Dynamic FM missing exception wrapper

TRY/CATCH cx_root added

10

ENDLOOP missing after ENDTRY

Loop/try nesting structure error

ENDLOOP added in correct position

Key Technical Takeaways

  • The SPRO Monitor Configuration (Steps 8.1 and 8.2) is mandatory. The ABAP objects alone produce nothing visible. The FM must be registered against the object class and assigned to a monitor.
  • SSF_OPEN must be called once before the loop, not inside it. This creates one consolidated spool job rather than N separate jobs.
  • Dynamic SmartForm invocation via CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name requires TRY/CATCH cx_root. Standard EXCEPTIONS clauses do not catch all runtime errors from dynamically resolved FM names.
  • Control parameters (ls_ctrl_para) must be fully populated — including langu and device — before SSF_OPEN. Both fields cause non-obvious runtime errors if blank.
  • Material ID to material number resolution in S/4HANA 2022 EWM uses /SAPAPO/MATKEY. Verify this table exists and is populated in your release before using this approach.
  • SmartForm barcode nodes referencing GS1 number ranges will fail at runtime in systems without a GS1 company prefix. Remove or disable in development and sandbox systems.

 

System & Release Information

Developed and validated on: SAP S/4HANA 2022 (FPS00), Embedded EWM

Cross-release applicability: The architecture and approach apply broadly to S/4HANA Embedded EWM.

However, field names, FM parameters, and internal table types must be verified in your target release.

SCM-based (Decentralized) EWM 9.x has different BAdI and SPRO paths.

Closing Note

Building this enhancement required working through standard ABAP patterns, EWM-specific APIs, functional IMG configuration, and several release-specific behaviors that are not documented in SAP Help. The corrections table and the version disclaimers above reflect the honest record of that process — included because that knowledge has direct practical value for anyone attempting something similar on S/4HANA EWM.

If you have built similar EWM Monitor extensions or encountered different behaviors on other releases, please share in the comments.

Stay Connected:

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ayogesh

YouTube: www.youtube.com/@YogeshSCMArchitect

Happy Learning!

entry.title: Re: SAP PS Progress Tracking for Physical Weightage % and...
entry.content: Progress analysis requires costs to be planned by cost element and operations to be planned on activities. Without planned data, the system cannot calculate POC or earned value. This is a hard prerequisite. You can check the configurations related to POC based progress analysis.

entry.title: v价物料的价格差异没有被物料帐滚算到上层
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are on S/4HANA. After running CKMLCP for period 2026/05, the price variance of a V-price material is NOT distributed to consumption and ending inventory.

【物料信息】
- 物料: B121010000031
- 工厂: 2033 (assigned to company code 2030)
- 评估类型: (blank)
- 价格控制: V
- 价格确定: 2 (基于交易的)
- 差异金额: 2,048,181.38 CNY
- 期间: 2026/05

【已排查信息】
1. CKMLCP 5 steps (Selection→Preparation→Settlement→Posting Closing→Revaluation) all show green/success.
2. Factory 2033 is assigned to the costing run (company code 2030).
3. Selection variant has NO filter restrictions; also tried "Process again" option.
4. Material master: ML active, price determination = 2, no errors.

2. How to force CKMLCP to include and distribute the variance for this material?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model - Introducing the Remote Function
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members!

If you have been working with SAP Profitability and Performance Management for a while, you know that one of the platform's greatest strengths is its extensibility. In the On-Premise world, the Remote Function Adapter: External Function has long been a favorite among modelers who need to plug custom ABAP logic, written as a BAPI wrapper, directly into their calculation models. The idea is elegant: you write your logic once in ABAP, and SAP Profitability and Performance Management calls it, passes data in, and writes the output back to the result table.

For SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model customers, this raises a natural question: what about all that existing On-Premise ABAP logic? Rebuilding it natively in the Cloud is costly and unnecessary if it already works perfectly.  This gap is addressed by the General Function - a newly available function type in the Remote Function Adapter (RFA) palette in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model. It connects SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model to your On-Premise custom Function Module through an RFC destination configured in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

For On-Premise users: If you are already familiar with the Remote Function Adapter in SAP Profitability and Performance Management (On-Premise), you will immediately recognize the inspiration behind the General Function. The concept is the same. For those new to the On-Premise Remote Function Adapter, we recommend this blog post as background reading: Exploring SAP Profitability and Performance Management's Remote Function Adapter (RFA).

Prerequisites

1. Custom Function Module

First, you need to have a custom Function Module (commonly referred to as a BAPI Wrapper when it wraps a standard SAP BAPI) ready on your On-Premise SAP system. This is the On-Premise half of the connection - SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model calls it via RFC and reads the results back.

For the full list of what your custom Function Module must include to be compatible, refer to the Custom Function Module Compatibility Requirements section below. For a step-by-step guide on building one, refer to: Exploring SAP Profitability and Performance Management's Remote Function Adapter (RFA).

2. Cloud Connector and BTP Destination Setup

Now, in order to connect your On-Premise system to SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model, follow these steps:

  1. Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to map your On-Premise SAP system to your BTP subaccount, and add your BAPI as an allowed RFC resource within that system mapping.
  2. Create an RFC Destination in the SAP BTP Destination Service pointing to that On-Premise system. This is what will appear in the RFC Destination dropdown when configuring the General Function.

For a step-by-step guide covering both the Cloud Connector and SAP BTP destination setup, refer to Integration Setup: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud to use RFA FI-GL and FI-GLI to post to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Meet the General Function in the Remote Function Adapter (RFA) Palette

Open the modeling palette in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model and scroll to the Remote Function Adapter (RFA) group. You will find the General Function listed there alongside other RFA-type functions.

The icon is intentionally familiar to On-Premise users. It is reused from the SAP Profitability and Performance Management (On-Premise) RFA External Function, making it immediately recognizable if you have worked with that feature before.

One key structural difference worth knowing upfront: in SAP Profitability and Performance Management (On-Premise), you define Rules as child nodes beneath the External Function. The General Function in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model has no child nodes. All custom logic lives inside the BAPI wrapper on the On-Premise side. SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model is the orchestrator and the consumer of the result, not the rule engine.

Configuring the General Function

Drag and drop the General Function from the palette into your modeling diagram. Open its Properties panel and configure your Function ID and Description to identify the function within your model. The two sections that matter most for getting the function working are Specific and Signature.

Specific Section: Defining the RFC Connection

This is where you connect SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model to your On-Premise system.

  • RFC Destination (required, dropdown): Select the RFC destination that points to the On-Premise system where your custom Function Module is deployed. This list is populated from the RFC destinations configured in your SAP BTP tenant's Destination Service.

Important: If your target RFC destination does not appear in the dropdown, it has not yet been configured in the SAP BTP Destination Service. Do not proceed with configuration until this is resolved - the connection is a prerequisite for successful execution.

  • Function Name (free-text input): Enter the exact name of your custom Function Module (e.g., Z_TEST_EXTERNAL_FUNCTION). This field does not validate or suggest function names. You need to confirm the correct name with your ABAP team in advance. An incorrect name will only surface as a failure at runtime.

Signature Section: Why It Exists, and What It Does

Before diving into what you configure here, it helps to understand why this section exists at all. If you have worked with the SAP Profitability and Performance Management (On-Premise) External Function, you may have noticed that it does not require you to declare a Signature in the same way. In On-Premise, the Rules tab automatically discovers and displays the function module's interface through its Template section once you specify the Function Name, you can see the structure directly. The General Function takes a deliberate different approach: rather than auto-discovering the full interface, the Signature lets you explicitly declare exactly which fields to send to and receive from the custom Function Module. That is exactly what the Signature section is for. It is your way of telling SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model: "Here is the shape of the data the custom Function Module expects to receive, and here is the shape of the data it will return." Once you define this, SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model knows what columns to send through IT_DATA and what columns to read back from ET_DATA.

  • Selection Fields: Correspond to the fields in the BAPI wrapper's import structure (inside IT_DATA). These define the input columns SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model passes to the On-Premise function.
  • Action Fields: Correspond to the fields in the custom Function Module's export structure (inside ET_DATA). The values returned through these fields are written back to the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model result table.

Because SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model relies entirely on what you declare here, the field IDs and data types must match the custom Function Module's structure exactly, one-to-one. There is no automatic discovery or reconciliation. If the Signature says a field exists and the custom Function Module does not have it or vice versa, the connection breaks.

No Mapping Section: The General Function has no dedicated Mapping section in its Properties panel, unlike other RFA functions in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model, which include a mapping layer between SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model fields and BAPI parameters. For the General Function, the Signature is the mapping. Field names in the ABAP structures and field IDs in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model Signature must be identical - there is no UI layer in between to translate or reconcile differences.

Custom Function Module Compatibility Requirements

For the General Function to work, your custom Function Module on the On-Premise system must meet the following requirements.

  • Remote-Enabled Module set in the Attributes tab in SE37; makes the Function Module callable from outside the On-Premise system via RFC.

  • IT_DATA import parameter typed against a custom table type; SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model delivers input records through this parameter. The import structure's fields define what input columns SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model passes in. These must match the Selection Fields you declare in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model Signature section. The structure must also include __GUID__ so SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model can stamp each row with a tracking number before sending it.

  • ET_DATA export parameter typed against a custom table type; your Function Module writes output records here for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model to read back into the result table. The export structure's fields must match the Action Fields in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model Signature section. __GUID__ must also be present in this structure and populated for every output row, so SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model can match results back to their source records.

  • ET_MESSAGE export parameter; used to return log messages that appear in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model Application Log after execution.

Parameter names are fixed: IT_DATA, ET_DATA, and ET_MESSAGE cannot be renamed. The back end of the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model is built to look for these exact parameter names. If any are missing or renamed, the connection will fail at runtime.

Activating, Running, and Verifying the Result

Once your SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model configuration is complete and your custom Function Module is deployed on the On-Premise system, save and activate the General Function. The activation log will confirm that both the input function and the General Function are activated.

The General Function can then be run through all standard SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model run entry points: direct Run from the Modeling screen, Run with Parameters and Selections, Process Template, Process Instance, or Process Scheduler for automated runs.

After the run completes, open the Application Monitor to review the outcome. The logs show the full run chain: how many input records were selected, whether the RFC call was submitted successfully, how many records were processed, and any custom messages returned by the custom Function Module through ET_MESSAGE.

To verify the results, navigate to Manage Data and open the result table for your environment. The Action Fields defined in the Signature section will be populated with the output values returned from the custom Function Module exactly as it exported them.

In our example, the Result Object column (mapped from CH_OBJKEY in ET_DATA) is where you can confirm if the end-to-end connection succeeded. A populated Result Object column means SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model successfully reached the On-Premise system, the custom Function Module run, and the output values were sent back and written to the result table. If this column is empty, the custom Function Module either did not run or did not write any rows to ET_DATA. Check the Application Monitor for details.

Notice that in this example, the Result Object column is populated on every other row. This is expected behavior based on the sample code: the function only writes a result row when the loop index is an even number. In a real implementation, your custom Function Module's business logic determines which rows produce output; the structure of the result table will reflect exactly what it wrote to ET_DATA.

This is the complete end-to-end flow: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model passed the data in through IT_DATA, the On-Premise custom Function Module processed it, and the output came back through ET_DATA and landed in the result table.

Wrapping Up

The General Function is a meaningful step forward for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model customers who want to leverage existing On-Premise ABAP logic without rebuilding it from scratch in the cloud. Whether you have a complex allocation rule, a proprietary enrichment process, or a calculation that lives better on the On-Premise side, the General Function gives you a clean, supported way to call it from SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model and consume its results.

We are eager to hear from you. What use cases are you thinking about? What On-Premise logic would you most want to bridge into SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model? Share your thoughts in the comments below and stay tuned for the general availability announcement.

As always, feel free to reach out via the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Community  with questions.

Related Resources

  • Exploring SAP Profitability and Performance Management's Remote Function Adapter (RFA) foundational blog on the On-Premise External Function that inspired the General Function
  • Integration Setup: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud to use RFA FI-GL and FI-GLI to post to SAP S/4HANA Cloud step-by-step guide for configuring RFC destinations in BTP

entry.title: Re: v价物料的价格差异没有被物料帐滚算到上层
entry.content:
Hello @YiLe,

Your materials are configured with Price Determination 2 (Transaction-Based) and Price Control V (Moving Average Price). This means SAP adjusts the material price immediately at each goods movement. In CKM3N, open the Price History view instead of the Price Determination Structure to see how the moving average price evolved during the period.
CKMLCP provides an alternative way to revaluate based on received price differences during the period, and allocate these price differences into Consumption and Ending Inventory at the period end by running CKMLCP.  MAP and CKMLCP are two different options SAP provides to revaluate materials. They can't be mixed up. You have to choose MAP or CKMLCP. If you select the latter, please set the relevant material as 3S (by using CKMM, etc.)

For more, see https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/actual-costing-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/14199071

Thanks!
Grace

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 08 - CXL case-when-then operator
entry.content:
Within the CDS family of languages we have CXL, the CDS Expression Language. In the context of CDS models and queries, CXL brings the ability to use expressions for describing calculations, declaring conditions, and more, all of which enriches the CDS model and helps us focus on the domain model.

In CXL we think about path expressions, functions, literals and operators.

Operators are often referred to according to the number of operands they take:

  • unary operators take 1 operand (e.g. not to negate something)
  • binary operators take 2 operands (e.g. + to add two values)

There's an operator supported by CXL referred to as case-when-then, which expands in full syntax to case when ... then ... else ... end (there's a possibility to repeat the when ... then ... structure too, but that's beside the point here).

The pure form of this operator takes three operands (a condition, and two alternative values), and also has a supported syntactical equivalent which is found in many other languages such as JavaScript, and is often written as ?: for short.

If "unary" is the name for an operator that takes one operand, "binary" for one that takes two, what is the name for an operator that, like case-when-then, takes three?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Failed to create Live Connection at SAP SAC to SAP HANA Cloud for FinOps

entry.title: Re: Cancellation of Customer Return Delivery in EWM
entry.content:
Options for reversing/canceling in EWM

1) Goods Receipt (GR) Cancellation
Use /SCWM/PRDI to reverse the GR posting. This corrects wrong quantities, damaged materials, or incorrect batches. After reversal, you can repost the correct GR.

2) Warehouse Task/Order Reversal
If stock was put away incorrectly, cancel the warehouse task (WT) or reverse the warehouse order. Recreate the correct putaway task to maintain stock accuracy.

3) ERP-EWM Document Mapping
Activate BC Set /N/SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_RETRUN in ERP. Map ERP document types and item types to EWM equivalents. This allows proper handling of customer returns through ERP-EWM integration.

4) Blocked or Quality Stock Handling
If material is damaged or incorrect, move it to Quality Inspection or Blocked Stock instead of unrestricted use. This prevents incorrect stock from being available for outbound deliveries.

entry.title: Re: Electricity resale. Negative PUP price.
entry.content:
Possible Design Approaches

1. Use Account Assignment Category in PO
Assign the PO directly to the sales order (or COPA segment). When the delayed price difference hits, ML can settle it directly to the sales order/CO-PA. This requires tight integration of MM and SD documents.

2. Material Ledger with Actual Costing + Cost Object
Instead of treating electricity as stock, treat it as a cost object (sales order item or internal order). ML differences are then settled to that cost object, which flows into COPA. This avoids the “not distributed” bucket.

3. Valuated Sales Order Stock
Configure electricity as a valuated sales order stock material. Even though stock is zero, ML will track variances against the sales order. Differences are settled to COPA automatically.

4. Custom Enhancement (BAdI in ML/CO-PA Transfer)
SAP provides exits (e.g., BAdI_MLCCS_TRANSFER) to redirect “not distributed” differences. You can enhance logic to push differences to the correct COPA segment based on sales order reference. This is often used in industries with delayed pricing (utilities, commodities).

5. Price Determination Strategy
Use condition-based actual costing (e.g., provisional vs. final invoice price). Configure ML to treat the delayed invoice as a price adjustment rather than a GR reversal. This way, the adjustment flows through to the original consumption.
